questions.question: According to the Atkinson-Shiffrin model, what is the correct sequence of memory stages?

options.text: Long-term memory, short-term memory, sensory memory
Sensory memory, short-term memory, long-term memory
Short-term memory, sensory memory, long-term memory

questions.explanation: The Atkinson-Shiffrin model describes information flowing from sensory memory (brief initial recording) to short-term memory (limited capacity, brief duration) to long-term memory (virtually unlimited capacity, potentially permanent storage).

questions.question: A student connects a new psychology term to a personal experience from their own life in order to remember it better. This strategy is an example of:
questions.explanation: Elaborative rehearsal involves thinking about the meaning of new information and connecting it to existing knowledge or personal experiences. This produces deeper encoding than maintenance rehearsal (simple repetition) and results in more durable long-term memories.
